acceptanceCriteria: The Pi plugin no longer registers a top-level Babysitter /resume command that conflicts with Pi's built-in session resume command.
The Pi plugin still registers /babysitter:resume and forwards it to the Babysitter resume skill.
Other top-level Babysitter Pi aliases remain registered as before unless they are explicitly listed in reservedCommands.
The Pi README documents /babysitter:resume instead of advertising /resume as a Babysitter quick command.
A focused regression test or generated bundle check proves the Pi extension output skips top-level resume while retaining babysitter:resume.
Plugin generation, extension-mux tests/build, metadata verification, and diff hygiene checks pass or report exact environmental blockers.

knownIssueContext.title: Pi plugin: /resume conflicts with Pi's built-in session resume command
knownIssueContext.reportedFailure: After installing @a5c-ai/babysitter-pi, Pi startup warns that extension command '/resume' conflicts with a built-in interactive command and skips the Babysitter alias in autocomplete.
knownIssueContext.legacyPathNote: The issue body references plugins/babysitter-pi/extensions/index.ts, but triage says the live source in this checkout is the unified per-harness Pi overlay.
nonGoals: Do not remove the resume skill.
Do not change Pi's built-in /resume behavior.
Do not broadly redesign command registration across other harnesses.
